# ace-overlay

Overlay [Ace Editor](https://ace.c9.io/) on normal `input` / `textarea` elements.

When a user clicks on an `input` or `textarea`, an Ace Editor is launched on top of it. The editor is automatically destroyed when it loses focus, syncing its value back to the original element.

## Installation

```bash
npm install ace-overlay
```

## Requirements

The global `ace` object must be available (load Ace Editor separately).

```html
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/ace/1.32.0/ace.js"></script>
```

## Usage

### `activate(container, opt)` — recommended

Automatically attaches click-to-overlay behavior to all `input` and `textarea` elements inside a container.

```js
import { activate } from "ace-overlay";

const container = document.querySelector("#myForm");
activate(container, { base: document.body });
```

Use `dynamic: true` to also handle elements added to the DOM after the initial call (polls every 100ms).

```js
activate(container, { dynamic: true, base: document.body });
```

### `overlay(element, opt)` — direct control

Immediately overlays an Ace Editor on a specific element. Returns the editor instance.

```js
import { overlay } from "ace-overlay";

const editor = overlay(document.querySelector("textarea"), {
    base: document.body,
    oncreate({ editor }) {
        editor.session.setMode("ace/mode/javascript");
    },
    ondestroy({ editor }) {
        console.log("editor closed");
    },
});
```

## API

### `activate(container, opt)`

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `container` | `Element` | The container element to search within |
| `opt.base` | `Element` | Reference element for overlay positioning (default: `document.body`) |
| `opt.dynamic` | `boolean` | When `true`, polls every 100ms to handle dynamically added elements |
| `opt.oncreate` | `function` | Callback fired after the editor is created: `({ editor })` |
| `opt.ondestroy` | `function` | Callback fired before the editor is destroyed: `({ editor })` |

Returns the interval ID (from `setInterval`) when `dynamic` is `true`, otherwise `undefined`.

---

### `activate1(base, element, opt)`

Attaches a click listener to a single `input` or `textarea`. Has no effect if the element has already been activated (tracked via the `acted` symbol).

---

### `overlay(element, opt)`

Overlays an Ace Editor on top of the given element.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `element` | `HTMLInputElement \| HTMLTextAreaElement` | The element to overlay |
| `opt.base` | `Element` | Reference element for overlay positioning (default: `document.body`) |
| `opt.oncreate` | `function` | Callback fired after the editor is created: `({ editor })` |
| `opt.ondestroy` | `function` | Callback fired before the editor is destroyed: `({ editor })` |

Returns the Ace `Editor` instance.

**Behavior:**
- The overlay inherits the element's position, size, and font size
- Keystrokes are synced back to `element.value` in real time (dispatches `"input"` events)
- On blur, a `"change"` event is dispatched, the editor is destroyed, and the overlay `<div>` is removed from the DOM
- For `<input>` elements, a single-line editor is used (newlines are suppressed)

---

### `rect(element, base?)`

Returns the position and size of `element` relative to `base`.

```js
import { rect } from "ace-overlay";
const { left, top, width, height } = rect(el, document.body);
```

---

### Symbols

| Symbol | Description |
|---|---|
| `acted` | Property key set on an element once `activate1` has attached a listener, preventing duplicate registration |
| `sym_editor` | Property key used to store the active Ace Editor instance on the overlaid element |

```js
import { sym_editor } from "ace-overlay";

const editor = element[sym_editor]; // get the live editor, if any
```
